metadata.title: Leetgrind Verification Plan
metadata.principle: Verification is planned, named, and reused instead of improvised per task.

verification.purpose: Validate TypeScript contracts across the workspace.
verification.evidence: Exit code 0 and no TypeScript errors.

verification.purpose: Validate package builds, server build, and Vite production build.
verification.evidence: Exit code 0 and successful package plus web build output.

verification.purpose: Run Vitest unit, integration, and component tests.
verification.evidence: Exit code 0 and all test files passed.

verification.purpose: Run Playwright browser smoke and critical flow tests.
verification.evidence: Exit code 0 and all browser tests passed.

verification.purpose: Generate coverage report for review when module behavior stabilizes.
verification.evidence: Coverage report generated in coverage output.

verification.purpose: Run local server and web app for manual verification.
verification.evidence: Web app responds on localhost and API health endpoint returns ok.

scenarios.scenario: The app shell renders without runtime errors.
Routes remain type-safe after navigation changes.
UI state does not store persistent domain data.
Visible app UI can render in Russian and English through the localization layer.
GET /health returns ok.
tRPC procedures validate inputs through Zod or typed schemas.
Server routes coordinate use cases instead of embedding all business rules.
Domain package has no framework or provider SDK imports.
Domain concepts remain stable and explicit across feature work.
Schema changes preserve evidence-based progress tracking.
Vector fields and dimensions are intentional and documented.
Repository APIs do not leak raw persistence details unnecessarily.
Provider-specific details stay behind AiProvider or documented adapter contracts.
Structured outputs are validated before being trusted by product workflows.
Codex subscription auth is not treated as a universal API replacement.
Agent inputs and outputs are typed and explicit.
Agent results can produce traceable evidence or recommendations.
Agent workflows use retrieval context with citations when RAG is involved.
Chunking preserves source metadata.
Retrieval distinguishes content memory from user progress memory.
Retrieved context can be traced back to stored sources.
Every runner reports stdout, stderr, pass/fail, and duration.
Every runner has timeout handling before production use.
AI review consumes deterministic test results instead of replacing them.
Review scheduling is deterministic for the same input.
Low quality answers reset or shorten the review interval.
Shared UI components are presentation-only.
Components remain reusable and accessible.
Shared UI primitives do not hard-code product copy that should be localized by app callers.
Shared package contains cross-package contracts, not feature-specific implementation.

evidence-policy.rule: Final agent responses must state which verification commands ran and whether they passed.
If a command cannot run, the response must state the blocker and residual risk.
For UI changes, include manual or browser verification when feasible.
For AI workflow changes, include structured output validation and provider fallback considerations.
For persistence changes, include schema and migration impact.
For behavior changes, include tests at the lowest useful level and run pnpm test.
For critical route or browser workflow changes, run pnpm test:e2e when feasible.
For user-facing UI copy changes, include Russian and English localization coverage or state why it is not applicable.
